steps.title: Test the added cue claim
steps.content: Ortega and Shah concluded that carbon dioxide does not serve as an additional cue. The supporting finding should show that adding carbon dioxide to frog calls did not improve the trap's ability to attract midges.

steps.title: Reject carbon dioxide support
steps.content: Choice A says most midges were in the carbon dioxide trap. Choice B links trap entries to periods of carbon dioxide release. Choice D says higher carbon dioxide attracted more midges. All three make carbon dioxide look useful.

steps.title: Support calls alone
steps.content: If the calls-only trap attracted more midges than the calls-plus-carbon-dioxide trap, then carbon dioxide did not add a locating cue. The answer is C.
